openstack.cloud.address_scope.short_description: Create or delete address scopes from OpenStack
openstack.cloud.address_scope.description: Create or Delete address scopes from OpenStack.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the resource

name.description: Name to be give to the address scope This option cannot be updated.

project.description: Unique name or ID of the project. This option cannot be updated.

ip_version.description: The IP version of the subnet 4 or 6. This option cannot be updated.

is_shared.description: Whether this address scope is shared or not.

extra_specs.description: Dictionary with extra key/value pairs passed to the API

openstack.cloud.application_credential.description: Create or delete an OpenStack Identity (Keystone) application credential. When the secret parameter is not set a secret will be generated and returned in the response. Existing credentials cannot be modified so running this module against an existing credential will result in it being deleted and recreated. This needs to be taken into account when the secret is generated, as the secret will change on each run of the module.

name.description: Name of the application credential.

secret.description: Secret to use for authentication (if not provided, one will be generated).

expires_at.description: Sets an expiration date for the application credential, format of YYYY-mm-ddTHH:MM:SS (if not provided, the application credential will not expire).

unrestricted.description: Enable application credential to create and delete other application credentials and trusts (this is potentially dangerous behavior and is disabled by default).

access_rules.description: List of access rules, each containing a request method, path, and service.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent. Application credentials are immutable so running with an existing present credential will result in the credential being deleted and recreated.

openstack.cloud.auth.short_description: Retrieve auth token from OpenStack cloud
openstack.cloud.auth.description: Retrieve auth token from OpenStack cloud

openstack.cloud.baremetal_deploy_templat.short_description: Create/Delete Bare Metal deploy template Resources from OpenStack
openstack.cloud.baremetal_deploy_templat.description: Create, Update and Remove ironic deploy templates from OpenStack.

extra.description: A set of one or more arbitrary metadata key and value pairs.

id.description: ID of the deploy template. Will be auto-generated if not specified.

name.description: Name of the deploy template. Must be formatted as a trait name (see API reference). Required when the deploy template is created, after which the name or ID may be used.

steps.description: List of deploy steps to apply. Required when the deploy template is created.

state.description: Indicates desired state of the resource

openstack.cloud.baremetal_inspect.short_description: Explicitly triggers baremetal node introspection in ironic.
openstack.cloud.baremetal_inspect.description: Requests Ironic to set a node into inspect state in order to collect metadata regarding the node. This command may be out of band or in-band depending on the ironic driver configuration. This is only possible on nodes in 'manageable' and 'available' state.

mac.description: unique mac address that is used to attempt to identify the host.

name.description: Name or id of the node to inspect. Mutually exclusive with I(mac)

openstack.cloud.baremetal_node.short_description: Create/Delete Bare Metal Resources from OpenStack
openstack.cloud.baremetal_node.description: Create or Remove Ironic nodes from OpenStack.

bios_interface.description: The bios interface for this node, e.g. C(no-bios).

boot_interface.description: The boot interface for this node, e.g. C(pxe).

chassis_id.description: Associate the node with a pre-defined chassis.

console_interface.description: The console interface for this node, e.g. C(no-console).

deploy_interface.description: The deploy interface for this node, e.g. C(iscsi).

driver.description: The name of the Ironic Driver to use with this node. Required when I(state) is C(present)

driver_info.description: Information for this node's driver. Will vary based on which driver is in use. Any sub-field which is populated will be validated during creation. For compatibility reasons sub-fields `power`, `deploy`, `management` and `console` are flattened.

id.description: ID to be given to the baremetal node. Will be auto-generated on creation if not specified, and I(name) is specified. Definition of I(id) will always take precedence over I(name).

inspect_interface.description: The interface used for node inspection, e.g. C(no-inspect).

management_interface.description: The interface for out-of-band management of this node, e.g. "ipmitool".

name.description: unique name identifier to be given to the resource.

network_interface.description: The network interface provider to use when describing connections for this node.

nics.description: A list of network interface cards, eg, C( - mac: aa:bb:cc:aa:bb:cc) This node attribute cannot be updated.

power_interface.description: The interface used to manage power actions on this node, e.g. C(ipmitool).

properties.description: Definition of the physical characteristics of this node Used for scheduling purposes

raid_interface.description: Interface used for configuring raid on this node.

rescue_interface.description: Interface used for node rescue, e.g. C(no-rescue).

resource_class.description: The specific resource type to which this node belongs.

skip_update_of_masked_password.description: Deprecated, no longer used. Updating or specifing a password has not been supported for a while.

state.description: Indicates desired state of the resource

storage_interface.description: Interface used for attaching and detaching volumes on this node, e.g. C(cinder).

timeout.description: Number of seconds to wait for the newly created node to reach the available state.

vendor_interface.description: Interface for all vendor-specific actions on this node, e.g. C(no-vendor).

openstack.cloud.baremetal_node_action.short_description: Activate/Deactivate Bare Metal nodes from OpenStack
openstack.cloud.baremetal_node_action.description: Deploy to Bare Metal nodes controlled by Ironic.

deploy.description: Indicates if the resource should be deployed. Allows for deployment logic to be disengaged and control of the node power or maintenance state to be changed.

config_drive.description: A configdrive file or HTTP(S) URL that will be passed along to the node.

instance_info.description: Definition of the instance information which is used to deploy the node.  This information is only required when I(state) is set to C(present) or C(on).

maintenance.description: Set node into maintenance mode. The power state as controlled with I(power) will not be changed when maintenance mode of a node is changed.

maintenance_reason.description: A string expression regarding the reason a node is in a maintenance mode.

name.description: Name or ID of the Bare Metal node.

power.description: A setting to allow power state to be asserted allowing nodes that are not yet deployed to be powered on, and nodes that are deployed to be powered off. I(power) can be C(present), C(absent), C(maintenance), C(on) or C(off).

state.description: Indicates desired state of the resource. I(state) can be C(present), C(absent), C(maintenance), C(on) or C(off).

timeout.description: Number of seconds to wait for the node activation or deactivation to complete.

openstack.cloud.baremetal_node_info.description: Retrieve information about Bare Metal nodes from OpenStack.

openstack.cloud.baremetal_port_info.description: Retrieve information about Bare Metal ports from OpenStack.

openstack.cloud.coe_cluster.short_description: Manage COE cluster in OpenStack Cloud
openstack.cloud.coe_cluster.description: Add or remove a COE (Container Orchestration Engine) cluster via OpenStack's Magnum aka Container Infrastructure Management API.

cluster_template_id.description: The template ID of cluster template. Required if I(state) is C(present).

discovery_url.description: URL used for cluster node discovery.

flavor_id.description: The flavor of the minion node for this cluster template.

is_floating_ip_enabled.description: Indicates whether created cluster should have a floating ip. Whether enable or not using the floating IP of cloud provider. Some cloud providers used floating IP, some used public IP, thus Magnum provide this option for specifying the choice of using floating IP. If not set, the value of I(is_floating_ip_enabled) of the cluster template specified with I(cluster_template_id) will be used. When I(is_floating_ip_enabled) is set to C(true), then I(external_network_id) in cluster template must be defined.

keypair.description: Name of the keypair to use.

master_count.description: The number of master nodes for this cluster. Magnum's default value for I(master_count) is 1.

master_flavor_id.description: The flavor of the master node for this cluster template.

name.description: Name that has to be given to the cluster template.

node_count.description: The number of nodes for this cluster. Magnum's default value for I(node_count) is 1.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the resource.

openstack.cloud.coe_cluster_template.short_description: Manage COE cluster template in OpenStack Cloud
openstack.cloud.coe_cluster_template.description: Add or remove a COE (Container Orchestration Engine) cluster template via OpenStack's Magnum aka Container Infrastructure Management API.

coe.description: The Container Orchestration Engine for this cluster template Required if I(state) is C(present).

dns_nameserver.description: The DNS nameserver address. Magnum's default value for I(dns_nameserver) is C(8.8.8.8).

docker_volume_size.description: The size in GB of the docker volume.

external_network_id.description: The external network to attach to the cluster. When I(is_floating_ip_enabled) is set to C(true), then I(external_network_id) must be defined.

fixed_network.description: The fixed network name or id to attach to the cluster.

fixed_subnet.description: The fixed subnet name or id to attach to the cluster.

flavor_id.description: The flavor of the minion node for this cluster template.

is_floating_ip_enabled.description: Indicates whether created clusters should have a floating ip or not. When I(is_floating_ip_enabled) is set to C(true), then I(external_network_id) must be defined.

is_master_lb_enabled.description: Indicates whether created clusters should have a load balancer for master nodes or not. Magnum's default value for I(is_master_lb_enabled) is C(true), ours is C(false).

is_public.description: Indicates whether the cluster template is public or not. Magnum's default value for I(is_public) is C(false).

is_registry_enabled.description: Indicates whether the docker registry is enabled. Magnum's default value for I(is_registry_enabled) is C(false).

insecure_registry.description: The URL pointing to users own private insecure docker registry.

is_tls_disabled.description: Indicates whether the TLS should be disabled. Magnum's default value for I(is_tls_disabled) is C(false).

keypair_id.description: Name or ID of the keypair to use.

image_id.description: Image id the cluster will be based on. Required if I(state) is C(present).

http_proxy.description: Address of a proxy that will receive all HTTP requests and relay them. The format is a URL including a port number.

https_proxy.description: Address of a proxy that will receive all HTTPS requests and relay them. The format is a URL including a port number.

master_flavor_id.description: The flavor of the master node for this cluster template.

name.description: Name that has to be given to the cluster template.

network_driver.description: The name of the driver used for instantiating container networks.

no_proxy.description: A comma separated list of IPs for which proxies should not be used in the cluster.

server_type.description: Server type for this cluster template. Magnum's default value for I(server_type) is C(vm).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the resource.

volume_driver.description: The name of the driver used for instantiating container volumes.

openstack.cloud.compute_flavor.description: Add or remove compute flavors from OpenStack. Updating a flavor consists of deleting and (re)creating a flavor.

disk.description: Size of local disk, in GB. Required when I(state) is C(present).

id.description: ID for the flavor. This is optional as a unique UUID will be assigned if a value is not specified. Note that this ID will only be used when first creating the flavor. The ID of an existing flavor cannot be changed. When I(id) is set to C(auto), a new id will be autogenerated. C(auto) is kept for backward compatibility and will be dropped in the next major release.

is_public.description: Make flavor accessible to the public.

name.description: Flavor name.

ram.description: Amount of memory, in MB. Required when I(state) is C(present).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the resource. When I(state) is C(present), then I(ram), I(vcpus), and I(disk) are required. There are no default values for those parameters.

vcpus.description: Number of virtual CPUs. Required when I(state) is C(present).

openstack.cloud.compute_flavor_access.short_description: Manage access to OpenStack compute flavors
openstack.cloud.compute_flavor_access.description: Add or remove access to OpenStack compute flavor

name.description: Name or ID of the compute flavor.

project.description: ID or Name of project to grant. Allow I(project) to access private flavor (name or ID).

project_domain.description: Domain the project belongs to (name or ID). This can be used in case collisions between project names exist.

state.description: Indicate whether project should have access to compute flavor or not.

openstack.cloud.compute_flavor_info.short_description: Fetch compute flavors from OpenStack cloud

ephemeral.description: Filter flavors based on the amount of ephemeral storage. I(ephemeral) supports same format as I(ram) option.

limit.description: Limits number of flavors to I(limit) results. By default all matching flavors are returned.

name.description: Flavor name.

ram.description: A string used for filtering flavors based on the amount of RAM (in MB) desired. This string accepts the following special values: 'MIN' (return flavors with the minimum amount of RAM), and 'MAX' (return flavors with the maximum amount of RAM). A specific amount of RAM may also be specified. Any flavors with this exact amount of RAM will be returned. A range of acceptable RAM may be given using a special syntax. Simply prefix the amount of RAM with one of these acceptable range values: '<', '>', '<=', '>='. These values represent less than, greater than, less than or equal to, and greater than or equal to, respectively.

vcpus.description: Filter flavors based on the number of virtual CPUs. I(vcpus) supports same format as I(ram) option.

binary.description: Filter the service list result by binary name of the service.

host.description: Filter the service list result by the host name.

openstack.cloud.config.description: Get OpenStack cloud credentials and configuration, e.g. from clouds.yaml and environment variables.

clouds.description: List of clouds to limit the return list to. When I(clouds) is not defined, then data is returned for all configured clouds.

openstack.cloud.dns_zone.description: Create, delete or update a OpenStack DNS zone.

email.description: Email of the zone owner. Only applies if I(type) is C(primary).

masters.description: Master nameservers Only applies if I(type) is C(secondary).

name.description: Name of the DNS zone.

state.description: Whether the zone should be C(present) or C(absent).

ttl.description: TTL (Time To Live) value in seconds.

type.description: Zone type. This attribute cannot be updated.

openstack.cloud.dns_zone_info.description: Getting information about dns zones. Output can be filtered.

email.description: Email of the zone owner Only applies to primary zones.

name.description: Zone name.

ttl.description: TTL (Time To Live) value in seconds.

openstack.cloud.endpoint.description: Create, update, or delete OpenStack Identity service endpoints. If a service with the same combination of I(service), I(interface) and I(region) exist, the I(url), I(enabled) and I(state) (C(present) or C(absent)) will be updated.

service.description: Name or id of the service.

region.description: ID of the region that the service belongs to. Note that I(region) is used for authentication.

state.description: Should the resource be C(present) or C(absent).

openstack.cloud.federation_idp.short_description: Manage an identity provider in a OpenStack cloud
openstack.cloud.federation_idp.description: Create, update or delete an identity provider of the OpenStack identity (Keystone) service.

description.description: The description of the identity provider.

domain_id.description: The ID of a domain that is associated with the identity provider. Federated users that authenticate with the identity provider will be created under the domain specified. Required when creating a new identity provider.

id.description: The ID (and name) of the identity provider.

is_enabled.description: Whether the identity provider is enabled or not. Will default to C(false) when creating a new identity provider.

remote_ids.description: List of the unique identity provider's remote IDs. Will default to an empty list when creating a new identity provider.

state.description: Whether the identity provider should be C(present) or C(absent).

id.description: The ID (and name) of the identity provider to fetch.

name.description: The name of the mapping to manage.

rules.description: The rules that comprise the mapping. These are pairs of I(local) and I(remote) definitions. For more details on how these work please see the OpenStack documentation U(https://docs.openstack.org/keystone/latest/admin/federation/mapping_combinations.html). Required if I(state) is C(present).

state.description: Whether the mapping should be C(present) or C(absent).

name.description: ID or name of the federation mapping.

openstack.cloud.floating_ip.short_description: Manage floating IP addresses for OpenStack servers
openstack.cloud.floating_ip.description: Add or remove an floating ip address to/from an OpenStack server. Returns the floating IP when attaching only if I(wait) is C(true). When detaching a floating IP there might be a delay until an server does not list the floating IP any more.

fixed_address.description: To which fixed IP of server the floating IP address should be attached to.

floating_ip_address.description: A floating IP address to attach or to detach. When I(state) is present can be used to specify a IP address to attach. I(floating_ip_address) requires I(network) to be set.

nat_destination.description: The name or id of a neutron private network that the fixed IP to attach floating IP is on

network.description: The name or ID of a neutron external network or a nova pool name.

purge.description: When I(state) is absent, indicates whether or not to delete the floating IP completely, or only detach it from the server. Default is to detach only.

reuse.description: When I(state) is present, and I(floating_ip_address) is not present, this parameter can be used to specify whether we should try to reuse a floating IP address already allocated to the project. When I(reuse) is C(true), I(network) is defined and I(floating_ip_address) is undefined, then C(nat_destination) and C(fixed_address) will be ignored.

server.description: The name or ID of the server to which the IP address should be assigned.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

openstack.cloud.floating_ip_info.description: Get a generator of floating ips.

description.description: The description of a floating IP.

fixed_ip_address.description: The fixed IP address associated with a floating IP address.

floating_ip_address.description: The IP address of a floating IP.

floating_network.description: The name or id of the network associated with a floating IP.

port.description: The name or id of the port to which a floating IP is associated.

project.description: The name or ID of the project a floating IP is associated with.

router.description: The name or id of an associated router.

status.description: The status of a floating IP.

openstack.cloud.group_assignment.description: Add and remove OpenStack identity (Keystone) users to/from groups.

availability_zone.description: Availability zone to create aggregate into.

purge_hosts.description: Whether hosts not in I(hosts) should be removed from the aggregate

openstack.cloud.image.short_description: Manage images of OpenStack image (Glance) service.
openstack.cloud.image.description: Create or delete images in OpenStack image (Glance) service.

container_format.description: The format of the container. This image attribute cannot be changed. Examples are C(ami), C(aki), C(ari), C(bare), C(ovf), C(ova) or C(docker).

disk_format.description: The format of the disk that is getting uploaded. This image attribute cannot be changed. Examples are C(ami), C(ari), C(aki), C(vhd), C(vmdk), C(raw), C(qcow2), C(vdi), c(iso), C(vhdx) or C(ploop).

filename.description: The path to the file which has to be uploaded. This image attribute cannot be changed.

id.description: The ID of the image when uploading an image. This image attribute cannot be changed.

is_public.description: Whether the image can be accessed publicly. Setting I(is_public) to C(true) requires admin role by default. I(is_public) has been deprecated. Use I(visibility) instead of I(is_public).

kernel.description: The name of an existing kernel image that will be associated with this image.

min_disk.description: The minimum disk space (in GB) required to boot this image.

min_ram.description: The minimum ram (in MB) required to boot this image.

name.description: The name of the image when uploading - or the name/ID of the image if deleting. If provided with the id, it can be used to change the name of existing image.

owner.description: The name or ID of the project owning the image.

owner_domain.description: The name or id of the domain the project owning the image belongs to. May be used to identify a unique project when providing a name to the project argument and multiple projects with such name exist.

properties.description: Additional properties to be associated with this image.

ramdisk.description: The name of an existing ramdisk image that will be associated with this image.

state.description: Should the resource be present, absent or inactive.

tags.description: List of tags to be applied to the image.

volume.description: ID of a volume to create an image from. The volume must be in AVAILABLE state. I(volume) has been deprecated. Use module M(openstack.cloud.volume) instead.

use_import.description: Use the 'glance-direct' method of the interoperable image import mechanism. Should only be used when needed, such as when the user needs the cloud to transform image format.

import_method.description: Method to use for importing the image. Not all deployments support all methods. Supports web-download or glance-download. copy-image is not supported with create actions. glance-direct is removed from the import method so use_import can be used in that case.

uri.description: Required only if using the web-download import method. This url is where the data is made available to the Image service.

openstack.cloud.image_info.description: Fetch images from OpenStack image (Glance) service.

filters.description: Dict of properties of the images used for query

openstack.cloud.keypair_info.description: Get information about keypairs that are associated with the account

name.description: Name or ID of the keypair

user_id.description: It allows admin users to operate key-pairs of specified user ID.

limit.description: Requests a page size of items. Returns a number of items up to a limit value.

name.description: ID or name of the federation protocol. This attribute cannot be updated.

idp.description: ID or name of the identity provider this protocol is associated with. This attribute cannot be updated.

mapping.description: ID or name of the mapping to use for this protocol. Required when creating a new protocol.

state.description: Whether the protocol should be C(present) or C(absent).

name.description: ID or name of the federation protocol.

idp.description: ID or name of the identity provider this protocol is associated with.

openstack.cloud.lb_health_monitor.short_description: Manage health monitor in a OpenStack load-balancer pool
openstack.cloud.lb_health_monitor.description: Add, update or remove health monitor from a load-balancer pool in OpenStack cloud.

delay.description: The interval, in seconds, between health checks. Required when I(state) is C(present).

expected_codes.description: The list of HTTP status codes expected in response from the member to declare it healthy. Specify one of the following values. For example, I(expected_codes) could be a single value, such as C(200), a list, such as C(200, 202) or a range, such as C(200-204). Octavia's default for I(expected_codes) is C(200).

health_monitor_timeout.description: The time, in seconds, after which a health check times out. Must be less than I(delay). Required when I(state) is C(present).

http_method.description: The HTTP method that the health monitor uses for requests. For example, I(http_method) could be C(CONNECT), C(DELETE), C(GET), C(HEAD), C(OPTIONS), C(PATCH), C(POST), C(PUT), or C(TRACE). Octavia's default for I(http_method) is C(GET).

is_admin_state_up.description: Whether the health monitor is up or down.

max_retries.description: The number of successful checks before changing the operating status of the member to ONLINE. Required when I(state) is C(present).

max_retries_down.description: The number of allowed check failures before changing the operating status of the member to ERROR. A valid value is from 1 to 10.

name.description: Name that has to be given to the health monitor. This attribute cannot be updated.

pool.description: The pool name or id to monitor by the health monitor. Required when I(state) is C(present). This attribute cannot be updated.

openstack.cloud.security_group_rule.short_description: Manage security group rules in OpenStack network (Neutron)
openstack.cloud.security_group_rule.description: Add or remove security group rule to/from OpenStack network (Neutron) service. Use I(security_group_rules) in M(openstack.cloud.security_group) to define a set of security group rules. It will be much faster than using this module when creating or removing several security group rules because the latter will do individual calls to OpenStack network (Neutron) API for each security group rule.

description.description: Description of the security group rule.

direction.description: The direction in which the security group rule is applied. Not all providers support C(egress).

ether_type.description: Must be IPv4 or IPv6, and addresses represented in CIDR must match the ingress or egress rules. Not all providers support IPv6.

port_range_max.description: The maximum port number in the range that is matched by the security group rule. If the protocol is TCP, UDP, DCCP, SCTP or UDP-Lite this value must be greater than or equal to the I(port_range_min) attribute value. If the protocol is ICMP, this value must be an ICMP code.

port_range_min.description: The minimum port number in the range that is matched by the security group rule. If the protocol is TCP, UDP, DCCP, SCTP or UDP-Lite this value must be less than or equal to the port_range_max attribute value. If the protocol is ICMP, this value must be an ICMP type.

project.description: Unique name or ID of the project.

protocol.description: The IP protocol can be represented by a string, an integer, or null. Valid string or integer values are C(any) or C(0), C(ah) or C(51), C(dccp) or C(33), C(egp) or C(8), C(esp) or C(50), C(gre) or C(47), C(icmp) or C(1), C(icmpv6) or C(58), C(igmp) or C(2), C(ipip) or C(4), C(ipv6-encap) or C(41), C(ipv6-frag) or C(44), C(ipv6-icmp) or C(58), C(ipv6-nonxt) or C(59), C(ipv6-opts) or C(60), C(ipv6-route) or C(43), C(ospf) or C(89), C(pgm) or C(113), C(rsvp) or C(46), C(sctp) or C(132), C(tcp) or C(6), C(udp) or C(17), C(udplite) or C(136), C(vrrp) or C(112). Additionally, any integer value between C([0-255]) is also valid. The string any (or integer 0) means all IP protocols. See the constants in neutron_lib.constants for the most up-to-date list of supported strings.

remote_group.description: Name or ID of the security group to link. Mutually exclusive with I(remote_ip_prefix).

remote_ip_prefix.description: Source IP address(es) in CIDR notation. When a netmask such as C(/32) is missing from I(remote_ip_prefix), then this module will fail on updates with OpenStack error message C(Security group rule already exists.). Mutually exclusive with I(remote_group).

security_group.description: Name or ID of the security group.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

openstack.cloud.security_group_rule_info.short_description: Fetch OpenStack network (Neutron) security group rules
openstack.cloud.security_group_rule_info.description: Fetch security group rules from OpenStack network (Neutron) API.

description.description: Filter the list result by the human-readable description of the resource.

direction.description: Filter the security group rule list result by the direction in which the security group rule is applied.

ether_type.description: Filter the security group rule list result by the ether_type of network traffic. The value must be IPv4 or IPv6.

id.description: Filter the list result by the ID of the security group rule.

project.description: Unique name or ID of the project.

protocol.description: Filter the security group rule list result by the IP protocol.

remote_group.description: Filter the security group rule list result by the name or ID of the remote group that associates with this security group rule.

remote_ip_prefix.description: Source IP address(es) in CIDR notation (exclusive with remote_group)

revision_number.description: Filter the list result by the revision number of the resource.

security_group.description: Name or ID of the security group

openstack.cloud.server.description: Create or Remove compute instances from OpenStack.

auto_ip.description: Ensure instance has public ip however the cloud wants to do that. For example, the cloud could add a floating ip for the server or attach the server to a public network. Requires I(wait) to be C(True) during server creation. Floating IP support is unstable in this module, use with caution. Options I(auto_ip), I(floating_ip_pools) and I(floating_ips) interact in non-obvious ways and undocumentable depth. For explicit and safe attaching and detaching of floating ip addresses use module I(openstack.cloud.resource) instead.

availability_zone.description: Availability zone in which to create the server. This server attribute cannot be updated.

boot_from_volume.description: Should the instance boot from a persistent volume created based on the image given. Mutually exclusive with boot_volume. This server attribute cannot be updated.

boot_volume.description: Volume name or id to use as the volume to boot from. Implies boot_from_volume. Mutually exclusive with image and boot_from_volume. This server attribute cannot be updated.

config_drive.description: Whether to boot the server with config drive enabled. This server attribute cannot be updated.

delete_ips.description: When I(state) is C(absent) and this option is true, any floating IP address associated with this server will be deleted along with it. Floating IP support is unstable in this module, use with caution.

flavor.description: The name or id of the flavor in which the new instance has to be created. Exactly one of I(flavor) and I(flavor_ram) must be defined when I(state=present). This server attribute cannot be updated.

flavor_include.description: Text to use to filter flavor names, for the case, such as Rackspace, where there are multiple flavors that have the same ram count. flavor_include is a positive match filter - it must exist in the flavor name. This server attribute cannot be updated.

flavor_ram.description: The minimum amount of ram in MB that the flavor in which the new instance has to be created must have. Exactly one of I(flavor) and I(flavor_ram) must be defined when I(state=present). This server attribute cannot be updated.

floating_ip_pools.description: Name of floating IP pool from which to choose a floating IP. Requires I(wait) to be C(True) during server creation. Floating IP support is unstable in this module, use with caution. Options I(auto_ip), I(floating_ip_pools) and I(floating_ips) interact in non-obvious ways and undocumentable depth. For explicit and safe attaching and detaching of floating ip addresses use module I(openstack.cloud.resource) instead.

floating_ips.description: list of valid floating IPs that pre-exist to assign to this node. Requires I(wait) to be C(True) during server creation. Floating IP support is unstable in this module, use with caution. Options I(auto_ip), I(floating_ip_pools) and I(floating_ips) interact in non-obvious ways and undocumentable depth. For explicit and safe attaching and detaching of floating ip addresses use module I(openstack.cloud.resource) instead.

image.description: The name or id of the base image to boot. Required when I(boot_from_volume=true). This server attribute cannot be updated.

image_exclude.description: Text to use to filter image names, for the case, such as HP, where there are multiple image names matching the common identifying portions. image_exclude is a negative match filter - it is text that may not exist in the image name. This server attribute cannot be updated.

key_name.description: The key pair name to be used when creating a instance. This server attribute cannot be updated.

metadata.description: A list of key value pairs that should be provided as a metadata to the new instance or a string containing a list of key-value pairs. Example:  metadata: "key1=value1,key2=value2"

name.description: Name that has to be given to the instance. It is also possible to specify the ID of the instance instead of its name if I(state) is I(absent). This server attribute cannot be updated.

network.description: Name or ID of a network to attach this instance to. A simpler version of the I(nics) parameter, only one of I(network) or I(nics) should be supplied. This server attribute cannot be updated.

nics.description: A list of networks to which the instance's interface should be attached. Networks may be referenced by net-id/net-name/port-id or port-name. Also this accepts a string containing a list of (net/port)-(id/name) Example: C(nics: "net-id=uuid-1,port-name=myport") Only one of I(network) or I(nics) should be supplied. This server attribute cannot be updated.

reuse_ips.description: When I(auto_ip) is true and this option is true, the I(auto_ip) code will attempt to re-use unassigned floating ips in the project before creating a new one. It is important to note that it is impossible to safely do this concurrently, so if your use case involves concurrent server creation, it is highly recommended to set this to false and to delete the floating ip associated with a server when the server is deleted using I(delete_ips). Floating IP support is unstable in this module, use with caution. This server attribute cannot be updated.

scheduler_hints.description: Arbitrary key/value pairs to the scheduler for custom use. This server attribute cannot be updated.

security_groups.description: Names or IDs of the security groups to which the instance should be added. On server creation, if I(security_groups) is omitted, the API creates the server in the default security group. Requested security groups are not applied to pre-existing ports.

state.description: Should the resource be C(present) or C(absent).

tags.description: A list of tags should be added to instance

terminate_volume.description: If C(true), delete volume when deleting the instance and if it has been booted from volume(s). This server attribute cannot be updated.

timeout.description: The amount of time the module should wait for the instance to get into active state.

userdata.description: Opaque blob of data which is made available to the instance. This server attribute cannot be updated.

volume_size.description: The size of the volume to create in GB if booting from volume based on an image. This server attribute cannot be updated.

volumes.description: A list of preexisting volumes names or ids to attach to the instance This server attribute cannot be updated.

wait.description: If the module should wait for the instance to be created.

openstack.cloud.server_action.short_description: Perform actions on OpenStack compute (Nova) instances
openstack.cloud.server_action.description: Perform actions on OpenStack compute (Nova) instances aka servers.

action.description: Action to perform. By default, only server owners and administrators are allowed to perform actions C(pause), C(unpause), C(suspend), C(resume), C(lock), C(unlock) and C(shelve_offload).

admin_password.description: Admin password for server to rebuild.

all_projects.description: Whether to search for server in all projects or the current project only.

image.description: Image name or ID the server should be rebuilt with.

name.description: Server name or ID.

openstack.cloud.server_group.description: Add or remove server groups from OpenStack.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the resource. When I(state) is C(present), then I(policy) is required.

name.description: Server group name.

policy.description: Represents the current name of the policy.

rules.description: Rules to be applied to the policy. Currently, only the C(max_server_per_host) rule is supported for the C(anti-affinity) policy.

openstack.cloud.server_info.short_description: Retrieve information about one or more compute instances
openstack.cloud.server_info.description: Retrieve information about server instances from OpenStack.

name.description: restrict results to servers with names or UUID matching this glob expression such as web*.

detailed.description: when true, return additional detail about servers at the expense of additional API calls.

filters.description:
Used for further filtering of results. Either a string containing a
JMESPath expression or a dictionary of meta data. Elements of the latter
may, themselves, be dictionaries.

all_projects.description: Whether to list servers from all projects or just the current auth scoped project.

openstack.cloud.server_metadata.short_description: Add/Update/Delete Metadata in Compute Instances from OpenStack
openstack.cloud.server_metadata.description: Add, Update or Remove metadata in compute instances from OpenStack.

name.description: Name of the instance to update the metadata

metadata.description: A list of key value pairs that should be provided as a metadata to the instance or a string containing a list of key-value pairs. Eg:  meta: "key1=value1,key2=value2" Note that when I(state) is C(true), metadata already existing on the server will not be cleared.

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

device.description: Device you want to attach. Defaults to auto finding a device name.

server.description: Name or ID of server you want to attach a volume to

state.description: Should the resource be present or absent.

volume.description: Name or id of volume you want to attach to a server

openstack.cloud.share_type.description: Add, remove or update share types in OpenStack Manila.

name.description: Share type name or id. For private share types, the UUID must be used instead of name.

extra_specs.description: Dictionary of share type extra specifications

is_public.description: Make share type accessible to the public. Can be updated after creation using Manila API direct updates.

driver_handles_share_servers.description: Boolean flag indicating whether share servers are managed by the driver. Required for share type creation. This is automatically added to extra_specs as 'driver_handles_share_servers'.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the resource.

openstack.cloud.share_type_info.description: Get share type details in OpenStack Manila. Get share type access details for private share types. Uses Manila API microversion 2.50 to retrieve complete share type information including is_default field. Safely falls back to basic information if microversion 2.50 is not supported by the backend. Private share types can only be accessed by UUID.

name.description: Share type name or id. For private share types, the UUID must be used instead of name.

openstack.cloud.stack.description: Add or Remove a Stack to an OpenStack Heat

environment.description: List of environment files that should be used for the stack creation

name.description: A name for the stack. The value must be unique within a project. The name must start with an ASCII letter and can contain ASCII letters, digits, underscores, periods, and hyphens. Specifically, the name must match the C(^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]{0,254}$) regular expression. When you delete or abandon a stack, its name will not become available for reuse until the deletion completes successfully.

parameters.description: Dictionary of parameters for the stack creation

state.description: Indicate desired state of the resource

tags.description: One or more simple string tags to associate with the stack. To associate multiple tags with a stack, separate the tags with commas. For example, C(tag1,tag2).

template.description: Path of the template file to use for the stack creation

timeout.description: Maximum number of seconds to wait for the stack creation

openstack.cloud.stack_info.description: Get information about Heat stack in OpenStack

name.description: Name of the stack.

owner.description: Name or ID of the parent stack.

project.description: Name or ID of the project.

status.description: Status of the stack such as C(available)

openstack.cloud.subnet.short_description: Add/Remove subnet to an OpenStack network
openstack.cloud.subnet.description: Add or Remove a subnet to an OpenStack network

state.description: Indicate desired state of the resource

allocation_pool_start.description: From the subnet pool the starting address from which the IP should be allocated.

allocation_pool_end.description: From the subnet pool the last IP that should be assigned to the virtual machines.

allocation_pools.description: List of allocation pools to assign to the subnet. Each element consists of a 'start' and 'end' value.

cidr.description: The CIDR representation of the subnet that should be assigned to the subnet. Required when I(state) is 'present' and a subnetpool is not specified.

disable_gateway_ip.description: The gateway IP would not be assigned for this subnet

dns_nameservers.description: List of DNS nameservers for this subnet.

extra_attrs.description: Dictionary with extra key/value pairs passed to the API

host_routes.description: A list of host route dictionaries for the subnet.

gateway_ip.description: The ip that would be assigned to the gateway for this subnet

ip_version.description: The IP version of the subnet 4 or 6

is_dhcp_enabled.description: Whether DHCP should be enabled for this subnet.

name.description: The name of the subnet that should be created. Although Neutron allows for non-unique subnet names, this module enforces subnet name uniqueness.

network.description: Name or id of the network to which the subnet should be attached Required when I(state) is 'present'

project.description: Project name or ID containing the subnet (name admin-only)

prefix_length.description: The prefix length to use for subnet allocation from a subnet pool

use_default_subnet_pool.description: Use the default subnetpool for I(ip_version) to obtain a CIDR.

subnet_pool.description: The subnet pool name or ID from which to obtain a CIDR

openstack.cloud.subnet_pool.description: Create, update or delete a subnet pool from OpenStack.

address_scope.description: ID or name of the address scope associated with this subnet pool.

default_prefix_length.description: The prefix length to allocate when the cidr or prefixlen attributes are omitted when creating a subnet.

default_quota.description: A per-project quota on the prefix space that can be allocated from the subnet pool for project subnets.

extra_specs.description: Dictionary with extra key/value pairs passed to the API.

is_default.description: Whether this subnet pool is the default.

is_shared.description: Whether this subnet pool is shared or not. This attribute cannot be updated.

maximum_prefix_length.description: The maximum prefix length that can be allocated from the subnet pool.

minimum_prefix_length.description: The minimum prefix length that can be allocated from the subnet pool.

prefixes.description: Subnet pool prefixes in CIDR notation.

project.description: Name or ID of the project.

image.description: Image name or id for boot from volume Mutually exclusive with I(snapshot) and I(volume)
